questions: Identify the statewide emergency medical service system in California that is administratively managed by the UCSF School of Pharmacy and dedicated to providing immediate expert treatment advice for poison exposures. Once identified, list the four distinct operational divisions (sites) that comprise this system. For each division, provide the name of the host hospital or medical center and the city in which it is located.

rounds.question: Identify the statewide emergency medical service system in California that is administratively managed by the UCSF School of Pharmacy and dedicated to providing immediate expert treatment advice for poison exposures. Once identified, list the four distinct operational divisions (sites) that comprise this system. rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the specific organization (California Poison Control System) based on its function and administrative management (UCSF School of Pharmacy). It then requires Wide Information Aggregation to locate and list the specific host institutions and locations for all four of its distributed operational sites, ensuring the agent retrieves a complete structural overview rather than a single fact.
source_nodes: Amazon.com: Poisoning and Drug Overdose, Eighth Edition eBook : Anderson, Ilene B., Benowitz, Neal L., Blanc, Paul D., Kim-Katz, Susan Y., Lewis, Justin C., Wu, Alan H. B., Olson, Kent R., Smollin, Craig: Kindle Store
Who We Serve | California Poison Control System
rounds.answer_a:
The California Poison Control System (CPCS) is a statewide emergency medical service system administratively managed by the UCSF School of Pharmacy, dedicated to providing 24-hour immediate expert treatment advice for poison exposures [1, 2]. Established in 1997, this unified network consolidates California's poison control services into a single toll-free number accessible to all 58 counties, handling over 300,000 inquiries annually from healthcare professionals and the public [1].

CPCS comprises four distinct operational divisions strategically located at university-affiliated medical centers throughout the state. The **Fresno Division** is hosted at Community Regional Medical Center in Fresno, serving California's central San Joaquin Valley region [3, 4]. The **Sacramento Division** operates from UC Davis Medical Center in Sacramento, covering northern California communities [3, 4]. The **San Diego Division** is based at UC San Diego Medical Center in San Diego, attending to southern California poison exposures [3, 4]. The **San Francisco Division** is located at Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital and Trauma Center in San Francisco, serving the Bay Area and northern coastal regions [3, 4].

Each division employs board-certified medical toxicologists, specially trained pharmacists, and registered nurses who deliver critical poison management consultations, reducing unnecessary emergency department visits and healthcare costs while improving patient outcomes [2].

## References
[1] California Poison Control System. (2023). About Us. UCSF School of Pharmacy. https://pharmacy.ucsf.edu/cpcs
[2] California Poison Control System. (2023). Services and Statistics. https://www.calpoison.org/about-us
[3] California Poison Control System. (2023). Our Centers. https://www.calpoison.org/about-us/our-centers
[4] California Poison Control System. (2023). Regional Divisions. https://www.calpoison.org/sites/default/files/2023-01/CPCS_Divisions.pdf
rounds.answer_b:
The statewide emergency medical service system in California administratively managed by the UCSF School of Pharmacy for immediate expert poison exposure treatment advice is the California Poison Control System (CPCS), a federally recognized regional network serving all state residents and healthcare providers 24/7 [1]. CPCS operates four distinct divisions to cover the state’s diverse, sprawling regions effectively. The first is the San Francisco Division, hosted by UCSF Medical Center in San Francisco, focusing on the Bay Area and adjacent northern counties [1]. Its second division, the Sacramento Division, is hosted by UC Davis Medical Center in Sacramento, serving northern California’s inland and rural areas beyond the Bay Area [1]. The third is the San Diego Division, hosted by UC San Diego Health – Hillcrest Medical Center in San Diego, addressing southern coastal and inland southern California communities [1]. The fourth is the Los Angeles Division, hosted by Children’s Hospital Los Angeles in Los Angeles, catering to the densely populated southern California region’s unique poison exposure needs [1]. Each division leverages local medical resources to deliver tailored, evidence-based advice for all poison-related emergencies.

## References
[1] "California Poison Control System (CPCS) | UCSF School of Pharmacy." University of California, San Francisco, https://pharmacy.ucsf.edu/california-poison-control-system. Accessed 26 Oct. 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identifies the four operational divisions of the California Poison Control System (Sacramento, San Francisco, Fresno/Madera, and San Diego). Agent B hallucinates a 'Los Angeles Division' (which does not exist in the current consolidated system) and fails to mention the Fresno division. Additionally, Agent B incorrectly identifies the host hospital for the San Francisco division as UCSF Medical Center, whereas Agent A correctly identifies it as Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital. Agent A follows the formatting constraints well and provides a much more accurate response.
